decisions.title: Use a centralized marketing content layer
decisions.decision: Keep reusable marketing copy, navigation, cards, and page content in content/site-content.ts.
decisions.reason: The site needs maintainable, CMS-friendly copy architecture without scattering text across many routes.
alternatives_considered: Introduce a full CMS before the MVP site was stable
decisions.impact: Makes copy and narrative changes faster and more consistent across pages.

decisions.title: Handle lead capture through a server-side Supabase route
decisions.decision: Public forms submit to /api/leads, which validates payloads and inserts into public.inbound_leads using the service-role key on the server.
decisions.reason: This keeps secrets server-side and preserves a clean, admin-friendly lead table.
decisions.impact: Secure lead capture is live and can be expanded later for CRM workflows.

decisions.title: Use the apex domain as canonical host
decisions.decision: roofingrecyclers.com is the canonical public host, with www redirected to the apex domain.
decisions.reason: This keeps brand presentation and SEO canonicalization straightforward.
alternatives_considered: Use www as the primary canonical host
decisions.impact: Production routing, metadata, and middleware are aligned around the apex domain.

decisions.title: Frame public investor and policy copy carefully
decisions.decision: The site should present RAS, regulatory, manufacturer, and relationship advantages in strong but careful language without making unverified public claims.
decisions.reason: The site needs to feel credible and ambitious while avoiding overstatement.
alternatives_considered: Use more aggressive pitch-deck language directly on the public site
decisions.impact: Investor-facing copy is more durable for a public audience and easier to defend.

decisions.title: Adopt docs/project-memory as canonical durable memory location
decisions.decision: Initialize and maintain the project memory system inside docs/project-memory within the repo.
decisions.reason: This location is local, versioned, easy to discover, and does not require separate mirroring for this repo.
decisions.impact: Future sessions can reconstruct project state directly from repo-backed JSON files.

decisions.title: Keep the home hero visual in a stacked layout
decisions.decision: The hero visual should avoid dense nested multi-column card layouts inside the constrained right-hand hero column.
decisions.reason: User feedback showed the previous nested layout made text look cramped and low-quality.
alternatives_considered: Keep nested side-by-side photo and card columns
Keep three mini-cards in a narrow row
decisions.impact: Home-page hero composition should remain roomier and more legible during future edits.

decisions.title: Version project memory immediately after initialization
decisions.decision: Commit and push docs/project-memory as soon as the initial memory system is created.
decisions.reason: The user's memory policy explicitly requires durable knowledge to be local-first and repo-backed, not left as untracked local files.
alternatives_considered: Leave memory files untracked until a later code change
decisions.impact: The memory system is now reconstructible from the repo and remote backup, not just the local workspace.

decisions.title: Use a simplified stacked composition for the home hero visual
decisions.decision: The home hero visual should use a full-width photo block, short thesis cards, and stacked operating-sequence rows instead of narrow nested text boxes.
decisions.reason: Direct user screenshot feedback showed the prior hero composition still looked cramped and low-quality even after earlier spacing tweaks.
alternatives_considered: Keep the previous hero structure and only reduce font sizes
Keep mini-cards and photo-overlay copy inside the constrained right-hand column
decisions.impact: The hero now has clearer hierarchy and more room for industrial/investor-grade copy without overflow-style composition problems.
